name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنْ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ إِذَآ أَنتُم بَشَرٌ تَنتَشِرُونَ (٢٠)
```

```english
Of His signs is that He created you from dust, then, behold, you are humans scattering [all over]! (20)
```

```arabic
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا لِّتَسْكُنُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهَا وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَكُم مَّوَدَّةً وَرَحْمَةً ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (٢١)
```

```english
And of His signs is that He created for you mates from your own selves that you may take comfort in them, and He ordained affection and mercy between you. There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect. (21)
```

```arabic
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ خَلْقُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَـٰفُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَأَلْوَٰنِكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّلْعَـٰلِمِينَ (٢٢)
```

```english
Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of your languages and colours. There are indeed signs in that for those who know. (22)
```

```arabic
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ مَنَامُكُم بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَٱبْتِغَآؤُكُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ (٢٣)
```

```english
And of His signs is your sleep by night and day, and your pursuit of His bounty. There are indeed signs in that for a people who listen. (23)
```

```arabic
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ يُرِيكُمُ ٱلْبَرْقَ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَيُحْىِۦ بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَآ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (٢٤)
```

```english
And of His signs is that He shows you the lightning, arousing fear and hope, and He sends down water from the sky, and with it revives the earth after its death. There are indeed signs in that for people who exercise their reason. (24)
```

```arabic
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَن تَقُومَ ٱلسَّمَآءُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِۦ ۚ ثُمَّ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ دَعْوَةً مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ إِذَآ أَنتُمْ تَخْرُجُونَ (٢٥)
```

```english
And of His signs is that the sky and the earth stand by His command, and then, when He calls you forth from the earth, behold, you will come forth. (25)
```

```arabic
وَلَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ كُلٌّ لَّهُۥ قَـٰنِتُونَ (٢٦)
```

```english
To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him. (26)
```

## (Commentary)

In this section, several verses are mentioned that demonstrate His Exaltedness’s oneness in Lordship (الربوبية) — *the exclusive authority of Allah to sustain, regulate, and govern all creation* — and Divinity (الألوهية) — *the unique right of Allah alone to be worshipped*. Reference is made therein to the interweaving and overlapping of creation (الخلق) and regulation (التدبير), so that it becomes clear that Lordship, in the sense of possessing the authority of regulation, and Divinity, in the sense of being the Truthful Object of Worship, are not deserved by anyone except Allah, Who created all things and brought them into existence. This is contrary to the claim of the idolater (الوثني) that creation belongs to Allah alone, while regulation and worship belong to the lords of the idols so that they may be intercessors for them with Allah, and that He, the Glorified, is merely the Lord of lords and the God of gods.

**His saying, the Exalted**: «وَمِنْ آياتِهِ أَنْ خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ تُرابٍ ثُمَّ إِذا أَنْتُمْ بَشَرٌ تَنْتَشِرُونَ» **"Of His signs is that He created you from dust, then, behold, you are humans scattering [everywhere]."** (30:20). What is meant by creation from dust is the ultimate origin of human creation from the earth; for the stages of human formation—whether from a fetus-lump (مضغة), a clinging mass (علقة), a drop of fluid (نطفة), or otherwise—are terrestrial compounds that trace back to earthly elements.

And His saying: «ثُمَّ إِذا أَنْتُمْ بَشَرٌ تَنْتَشِرُونَ» **"then, behold, you are humans scattering [everywhere]"**; the word "behold" (إِذا) indicates suddenness (فجائية), meaning it suddenly happens that you become human beings spreading across the earth. That is, He creates you from earthly compositions from which another similar dead earthly existence would be expected, yet suddenly and all at once, it becomes a human being possessed of life and intellectual perception (شعور عقلي), spreading through the earth to manage the affairs of life. Thus, His saying: «ثُمَّ إِذا أَنْتُمْ بَشَرٌ تَنْتَشِرُونَ» carries the same meaning as His saying: «ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْناهُ خَلْقاً آخَرَ» **"then We produced him as another creation"** (23:14).

Thus, the creation of man—meaning the gathering of his parts from the earth and their composition—is a sign (آية); and the transformation of this aggregate into a human being with life and intellectual perception is another sign, or multiple signs, pointing to a Living, All-Knowing Maker Who regulates the affair and conducts this wondrous system.

It has become clear through this meaning that "then" (ثُمَّ) denotes a rank-based sequence (التراخي الرتبي), and the clause is coordinated with His saying: «خَلَقَكُمْ» (He created you), not with the phrase «أَنْ خَلَقَكُمْ» (that He created you).

**His saying, the Exalted**: «وَمِنْ آياتِهِ أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَزْواجاً لِتَسْكُنُوا إِلَيْها» **"And of His signs is that He created for you mates from your own selves that you may take comfort in them"** (30:21) to the end of the verse. Al-Raghib said: "The term **mate** (زوج) is applied to each one of a pair, male and female, among animals that mate; and it is applied to each of any two companions, whether among animals or otherwise. The Exalted said: «فَجَعَلَ مِنْهُ الزَّوْجَيْنِ الذَّكَرَ وَالْأُنْثى» **'and made of him the two sexes, the male and the female'** (75:39), and He said: «وَزَوْجُكَ الْجَنَّةَ» **'and your mate, the Garden'** (2:35). The word *zawjah* (زوجة) is a poor linguistic usage, and its plural is *zawjat* (زوجات)..." until he said: "and the plural of *zawj* is *azwaj* (أزواج)." End quote.

So His saying: «أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَزْواجاً لِتَسْكُنُوا إِلَيْها» means He created for your sake—or for your benefit—companions from your own kind. This is because each of the man and the woman is equipped with a reproductive system whose function is completed only by the pairing with the other; through their union, the matter of procreation and lineage is fulfilled. Thus, each of them is deficient in themselves, in need of the other, and from the union, a complete single entity is formed capable of procreating. Because of this deficiency and need, each moves toward the other until, when joined, they find rest (سكن) in them; for every deficient thing yearns for its perfection, and every needy thing inclines toward that which removes its poverty. This is the desire (الشبق) implanted in each of these two companions.

And His saying: «وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ مَوَدَّةً وَرَحْمَةً» **"and He ordained affection and mercy between you."** **Affection** (المودة) is like love (الحب) whose effect is manifest in the realm of action. The relationship of affection to love is like the relationship of outward humility (الخضوع), whose effect is manifest in action, to inward submissiveness (الخشوع), which is a type of psychological state resulting from [perceiving] greatness and majesty.

And **mercy** (الرحمة) is a type of psychological state resulting from witnessing the deprivation of a deprived person from perfection and their need to have their deficiency removed; it calls the merciful one to save them from deprivation and rectify their lack.

Among the most prominent instances of affection and mercy is the domestic society (المجتمع المنزلي); for the two spouses are bound together by affection and love, and together—especially the wife—they show mercy to the young children because they see their weakness and inability to perform the necessary tasks to fulfill vital needs. Thus, they undertake the duty of protecting, guarding, feeding, clothing, sheltering, and raising them. Were it not for this mercy, lineage would be severed and the species would never survive.

A parallel to this affection and mercy is observed in the larger civil society (المجتمع المدني) among its members; for one individual finds sociability with another through affection and shows mercy to the poor, the disabled, and the weak who cannot fulfill the requirements of life.

The intended meaning of affection and mercy in the verse is either the former [domestic] sense, as suggested by the context, or the latter [civil] sense, as suggested by the generality of the verse.

And His saying: «لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ» **"There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect."** This is because if they reflect upon the ontological foundations (الأصول التكوينية) that drive man to form society—namely maleness and femaleness which call for domestic association, and affection and mercy which drive civil association—and then consider what results from this association in terms of the survival of the species and the perfection of man in his worldly and otherworldly life, they would discover such wonders of divine signs in the regulation of this species' affairs that would baffle their intellects and amaze their minds.

**His saying, the Exalted**: «وَمِنْ آياتِهِ خَلْقُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاخْتِلافُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَأَلْوانِكُمْ» **"Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of your languages and colours"** (30:22) to the end of the verse. It appears that the intended meaning of the difference of languages (الألسن) is the difference of tongues such as Arabic, Persian, Urdu, and others; and by the difference of colors (الألوان) is meant the difference of nations in their complexions, such as whiteness, blackness, yellowness, and redness.

It is also possible to derive the difference of languages from the perspective of tones, voices, and modes of speech and articulation; and the difference of colors as the difference of every two individuals among human beings in terms of color if scrutinized closely, as maintained by the scientists of this field.

Thus, those who investigate the macrocosm (العالم الكبير) discover in the system of creation subtle signs indicating that the making and bringing into existence, along with the system governing it, cannot be established except by Allah and does not terminate except in Him.

**His saying, the Exalted**: «وَمِنْ آياتِهِ مَنامُكُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ وَابْتِغاؤُكُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ» **"And of His signs is your sleep by night and day, and your pursuit of His bounty"** (30:23) to the end of the verse. **Bounty** (الفضل) is that which exceeds the amount of need; it is also applied to a gift because the giver only gives what is surplus to his own need. In the noble verse, it refers to sustenance (الرزق); thus, the pursuit of bounty is the seeking of sustenance.

In the creation of man with active powers that drive him to seek sustenance and fulfill the needs of life for survival through movement and effort, then guiding him to rest and stillness to remove the fatigue of effort and renew the equipment of his powers, and the designation of the alternating night and day for effort and rest, and the causation of the existence of night and day through celestial positions involving the earth and the sun—in all this are beneficial signs for one who possesses an attentive ear (سمع واع) that understands what it hears; for when he finds it to be the truth, he follows it.

The author of *al-Kashshaf* said regarding the verse: "This is of the category of involution (اللف) and its arrangement is: 'Of His signs is your sleep by night and your pursuit of His bounty by day,' except that He separated the first two paired elements with the latter two because they are times, and the time and what occurs within it are like a single thing, with the involution aiding the sense of unity. It is also permissible that your sleep is intended in both times and your pursuit in both of them, but the first view is more apparent due to its repetition in the Qur'an, and the soundest meanings are those indicated by the Qur'an." End quote.

From what has preceded, the meaning of concluding the verse with His saying: «إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ» **"There are indeed signs in that for a people who listen"** has become clear.

**His saying, the Exalted**: «وَمِنْ آياتِهِ يُرِيكُمُ الْبَرْقَ خَوْفاً وَطَمَعاً وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَيُحْيِي بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها» **"And of His signs is that He shows you the lightning, [evoking] fear and hope, and He sends down water from the sky, and with it He revives the earth after its death"** (30:24). It appears that the verb [يريكم] stands in the place of a verbal noun (المصدر); therefore, it was not preceded by the particle *an* (أن المصدرية) as were His sayings: «أَنْ خَلَقَكُمْ» and «أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُمْ». Treating a verb as a verbal noun is excellent Arabic usage, and upon this is based the well-known proverb: "Hearing of the Mu'aydi is better than seeing him." There is no harm in interpreting His speech, the Exalted, upon this, for He employs various modes of expression at the opening of these verses, such as His sayings: «مَنامُكُمْ» (your sleep), «يُرِيكُمُ» (He shows you), and «أَنْ تَقُومَ» (that [it] stands).

It has been suggested regarding His saying: «يُرِيكُمُ» that it involves the omission of the particle *an*, the implied meaning being "that He shows you the lightning," which is supported by the subjunctive reading (النصب) of *yuriyakum*.

It has also been suggested that it involves the omission of a prefixed noun (المضاف), the implied meaning being: "And of His signs is the sign of showing you the lightning." Another suggestion is that the implied meaning is: "And of His signs is the sign of lightning," then a new sentence begins: "He shows you the lightning..." It has also been suggested that «مِنْ آياتِهِ» is connected to His saying: «يُرِيكُمُ», the implied meaning being: "And He shows you the lightning from His signs." Finally, it has been suggested that «مِنْ آياتِهِ» is a circumstantial qualifier (حال) for the lightning, the implied meaning being: "And He shows you the lightning while the lightning is among His signs."

These are various views, and their far-fetched nature is not hidden from you, especially since some of them remove the speech in the verse from conformity with the context of the preceding similar verses, such as the last two views.

And His saying: «خَوْفاً وَطَمَعاً» means fear of the thunderbolt and hope for rain. And His saying: «وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَيُحْيِي بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها» has had its explanation repeated frequently. And His saying: «إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ» **"There are indeed signs in that for a people who exercise their reason"** means that the people of intellect understand that there is a Divine care (عناية) connected to these benefits, and it is not merely a matter of coincidence or chance (صدفة).

**His saying, the Exalted**: «وَمِنْ آياتِهِ أَنْ تَقُومَ السَّماءُ وَالْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِ ثُمَّ إِذا دَعاكُمْ دَعْوَةً مِنَ الْأَرْضِ إِذا أَنْتُمْ تَخْرُجُونَ» **"And of His signs is that the sky and the earth stand by His command. Then, when He calls you with a call from the earth, behold, you will come forth"** (30:25). **Standing** (القيام) is the opposite of sitting. Since it is the most balanced of human states, through which one is capable of most actions, it is used metaphorically for the stability of a thing and its settlement upon its most balanced state, just as it is used metaphorically for the regulation of an affair (تدبير الأمر). The Exalted said: «أَفَمَنْ هُوَ قائِمٌ عَلى كُلِّ نَفْسٍ بِما كَسَبَتْ» **"Is He who is the overseer of every soul as to what it earns [like those who are not]?"** (14:33).

What is meant by the standing of the heaven and the earth by a command from Allah is their stability in their state of motion, rest, change, and constancy by His command, the Exalted. He has defined His command by His saying: «إِنَّما أَمْرُهُ إِذا أَرادَ شَيْئاً أَنْ يَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ» **"All His command, when He wills something, is to say to it ‘Be,’ and it is"** (36:82).

And His saying: «ثُمَّ إِذا دَعاكُمْ دَعْوَةً مِنَ الْأَرْضِ إِذا أَنْتُمْ تَخْرُجُونَ»; the first "when" (إِذا) is conditional (شرطية), and the second "behold" (إِذا) is for suddenness (فجائية), standing in the place of the particle *fa* (فاء الجزاء) that introduces the result. The phrase "from the earth" (مِنَ الْأَرْضِ) is connected to His saying "a call" (دَعْوَةً). The sentence is coordinated with the position of the first sentence because the intended meaning of the sentence—namely His saying: «ثُمَّ إِذا دَعاكُمْ» etc.—is the Resurrection (البعث) and the return to Allah; it is not counted among the signs themselves, but rather the sentence is an announcement of a matter for which evidence was provided previously and will be provided hereafter.

As for the claim of one who says that the sentence is interpreted as a single noun and is coordinated with «أَنْ تَقُومَ», with the implied meaning being: "And of His signs is the standing of the heaven and the earth by His command, then your coming forth when He calls you with a call from the earth"—

This would necessitate that the Resurrection be counted among the signs, whereas it is not. Furthermore, the Resurrection is one of the three fundamental principles (الأصول الثلاثة) for which the signs are used as evidence; it is not itself used as evidence for Monotheism (التوحيد), for example. Rather, if evidence were to be used, it would be Monotheism as evidence for it. So understand that.

Since the mentioned signs—the creation of humans from dust, their creation as mates, the difference of their languages and colors, their sleep and pursuit of His bounty, the showing of lightning, and the sending down of water from the sky—are all signs pertaining to the regulation of human affairs, the intended meaning of His saying: «أَنْ تَقُومَ السَّماءُ وَالْأَرْضُ», aided by the context, is the stability of the heaven and the earth in their natural positions and normal states, being suitable for the life of the human species connected to them. His saying: «ثُمَّ إِذا دَعاكُمْ» etc., follows that in a sequential order of delay; that is, their coming forth from the earth is delayed after this "standing" and coincides with their destruction, as informed by many verses in various places of His speech, the Exalted.

It also becomes clear from this that the intended meaning of His previous saying: «وَمِنْ آياتِهِ خَلْقُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ» is their creation from the perspective of how they are connected to human life and benefit it.

The mentioned verses have been arranged starting from the beginning of human creation and formation, then his classification into two types: male and female, then the connection of his existence to the heaven and the earth and the difference of their languages and colors, then the effort in seeking sustenance and the stillness of sleep, then the showing of lightning and the sending down of rains, until it concludes with the standing of the heaven and the earth until an appointed term, so that the span of life decreed for this human species may be completed, followed by the Resurrection. This is part of the subtle points (النكات) in the arrangement of these signs.

The verse endings (الفواصل)—namely His sayings: "they reflect" (يَتَفَكَّرُونَ), "for the knowledgeable" (لِلْعالِمِينَ), "they listen" (يَسْمَعُونَ), and "they exercise reason" (يَعْقِلُونَ)—have been arranged in this order because man reflects, thereby becoming knowledgeable; then, when he hears something of the truths, he attends to it, and then he reasons it. And Allah knows best.

**His saying, the Exalted**: «وَلَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ كُلٌّ لَهُ قانِتُونَ» **"To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him"** (30:26). The mentioned signs were aimed at proving His Lordship and Divinity, as previously indicated. When the discourse reached the mention of the Resurrection and the return to Allah, it was followed by the proof of its possibility, with the argument being drawn from the creation and regulation mentioned in the previous verses.

So His saying: «وَلَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ» is an indication of the encompassment of His true ownership (الملك الحقيقي) over all who are in the heavens and the earth, who are those to be gathered unto Him. This is because their existence from every aspect is sustained by Him, the Exalted, in a state of poverty and need (فقر وحاجة); they have no independence or self-sufficiency from Him in any way. This is the true ownership whose effect is the permissibility of the Owner to dispose of His property as He wills; thus, it is for Him, the Exalted, to dispose of those He owns by transferring them from the worldly realm (النشأة الدنيا) to the hereafter (النشأة الآخرة).

He emphasized this with His saying: «كُلٌّ لَهُ قانِتُونَ» **"All are obedient to Him."** **Obedience** (القنوت) is the persistence in obedience (الطاعة) along with submissiveness (الخضوع)—as mentioned by al-Raghib in *al-Mufradat*. What is meant by obedience with submissiveness is ontological obedience (الطاعة التكوينية)—as suggested by the context—rather than legislative obedience (التشريعية), which might sometimes be violated.

This is because they are the angels, the jinn, and mankind. As for the angels, they possess nothing but the submissiveness of obedience. As for the jinn and mankind, they are obedient and submissive to the ontological causes and means (العلل والأسباب الكونية); whenever they attempt to nullify the effect of one ontological cause or means, they resort to another ontological cause and means. Furthermore, their knowledge and will, like their entire free choice, are among the ontological causes; thus, nothing occurs except what Allah wills—that is, that whose causes have been completed in external reality—and nothing of what they will is realized except that which He has permitted and willed. Thus, He is the Owner of them and of what they possess.
